cards.title: Enema — Types & Clinical Use
cards._legacyHtml:
Water Enema (Cleansing): 1–1.5 L lukewarm water (37°C). Bowel cleansing before procedures, acute constipation, toxic states. Retain 5–10 min.
Saline Enema: 1 tsp salt in 1 L water. Safer for electrolyte-sensitive patients. Less water absorption than plain water. Acute constipation, faecal impaction.
Neem Enema: Neem leaf decoction (10 leaves boiled in 1 L water, cooled). Anti-infective. Indications: intestinal parasites, amoebiasis, haemorrhoids, rectal infections. Retain 10–15 min.
Triphala Enema: 1 tsp Triphala powder boiled in 500 ml water, filtered, cooled to 37°C. Colon toning, chronic constipation, IBS, Crohn's (mild). Retain 10–15 min.
Dashamoola Enema (Basti): Ayurvedic decoction. Vatahara (Vata-pacifying). For: lower back pain, neurological conditions, constipation. Part of Panchakarma.

cards.title: Coffee Enema — Procedure & Evidence
cards._legacyHtml:
Preparation: 3 tbsp organic ground coffee (not instant) in 1 L distilled water. Boil 5 min, simmer 15 min, cool to 37°C, strain thoroughly.
Procedure: Left lateral position (Sims). Insert enema tip 7–10 cm into rectum. Administer 500 ml slowly over 5–10 min. Retain for 12–15 min. Evacuate.
Proposed mechanism: Palmitates in coffee → stimulate glutathione S-transferase (GST) in liver → phase II detoxification. Theophylline → bile duct dilation → bile flow ↑.
Clinical use in naturopathy: Gerson therapy (cancer adjunct), liver support, chronic fatigue, fibromyalgia, heavy metal detox support.
Frequency: 1–2 times/week. Maximum 1/day in acute protocols (only under supervision).
Contraindications: Ulcerative colitis, Crohn's disease, diverticulitis (acute), recent bowel surgery, cardiac conditions, caffeine sensitivity, pregnancy.

cards.title: Oil Enema (Sneha Basti)
cards._legacyHtml:
Type: Anuvasana Basti (oil retention enema). Classic Panchakarma treatment.
Oils used: Sesame oil (most common), Dashamoola taila, Mahanarayan taila, Dhanwantaram taila.
Preparation: 60–120 ml warm medicated oil (40–42°C).
Procedure: Left lateral position. Administer warm oil slowly. Retain as long as comfortable (minimum 30 min, ideally 3 hours or overnight). Gentle abdominal massage after administration.
Indications: Chronic constipation, Vata disorders, lower back pain, sciatica, neurological conditions, degenerative arthritis, debility, post-illness recovery.
Benefit: Lubricates and nourishes colon mucosa, pacifies Vata dosha, strengthens nervous system.
Frequency: Daily for 8 days in Panchakarma course. Once weekly for maintenance.
Contraindications: Acute diarrhoea, fever, indigestion, obesity (excess Kapha), rectal bleeding.

cards.title: Colonic Irrigation — Procedure
cards._legacyHtml:
Definition: Hydrotherapy of the colon using large volumes of water (20–60 L total) in multiple fill-release cycles under controlled pressure. Also called colonic hydrotherapy or high colonic.
Equipment: Colonic irrigation machine (gravity-fed or pump). Speculum, tubing (sterile/disposable). Temperature control: 37–38°C.
Procedure: 45–60 min session. Patient supine. Disposable speculum inserted. Alternating warm and cool water cycles (2–3 L per cycle × 10–20 cycles). Therapist massages abdomen during procedure. Closed system — waste exits through separate tube (no odour).
Indications: Chronic constipation, IBS (non-acute), pre-colonoscopy prep, detox protocols, candida overgrowth, colon preparation in Panchakarma.
Contraindications: Active IBD (Crohn's/UC flare), colorectal cancer, diverticulitis (acute), rectal bleeding, recent bowel surgery (<3 months), abdominal hernia, severe haemorrhoids, pregnancy, cardiac failure, renal failure.

cards.title: Colon Hydrotherapy — Protocols by Condition
cards._legacyHtml:
Chronic Constipation: 3–6 sessions over 3 weeks. Week 1: cleansing (plain water). Week 2–3: probiotic implant after final session (Lactobacillus acidophilus in water). Diet: fibre, hydration, flaxseed.
IBS-C (constipation-predominant): 4–6 sessions. Alternate plain and chamomile/peppermint tea enemas. Combine with low-FODMAP diet and probiotics.
Pre-Panchakarma (Virechana prep): 1–2 cleansing sessions. Plain lukewarm water. Done 1–2 days before Virechana administration.
Detox Programme: 3 sessions week 1, 2 sessions week 2, 1 session week 3. Alternate with coffee enema (only for robust patients). Combine with liver-support diet.
Post-session care: Rest 30 min. Warm soup or broth only for 2–3 hours. Probiotic supplementation after each session (Lactobacillus + Bifidobacterium). Avoid heavy meals for 4 hours.

cards.title: Colon Hydrotherapy — Contraindications & Safety
cards._legacyHtml:
Absolute contraindications:
• Active ulcerative colitis or Crohn's disease (flare)
• Colorectal cancer or rectal polyps
• Diverticulitis (acute) or diverticular abscess
• Rectal bleeding (any cause)
• Recent colorectal or abdominal surgery (<6 months)
• Severe haemorrhoids (Grade 3–4)
• Bowel perforation or fistula
• Pregnancy
• Renal failure, cardiac failure, liver failure
• Active severe infection (sepsis)

Relative contraindications:
• Abdominal hernia (use very low pressure)
• Mild anaemia
• Hypertension (controlled — monitor)

Safety standards: Use disposable speculums only. Water temperature 37–38°C (never above 40°C). Maximum pressure: gravity-fed preferred. Document sessions. Therapist must be trained and certified.
